TextENGNew: A clear, pristine blue sky stretched overhead.
From the sun floating within it, dazzling rays of light poured down into the classroom like a shower of brilliance.
changeReason: Old omits 'from the sun floating within it' (JPN そこに浮かんだ太陽から); new includes this detail about sunlight source.

TextENGNew: "Nice weather, huh... Nice, but..."
changeReason: Old: 'Lovely weather... Maybe too good though...'; new: 'Nice weather, huh... Nice, but...' JPN いい天気だねぇ…良すぎるけど = 'nice weather... too nice though'. Old adds 'lovely' and 'maybe' not matching JPN tone.

TextENGNew: The seasonal wardrobe change was already behind us, and the time had come for 'warm' to give way to 'hot.'
changeReason: Old: 'the climate was now officially that of hot, humid summer'; new: 'the time had come for what could only be called summer'. JPN says 衣替え (wardrobe change) was done and it's now 'summer' weather -- new is more accurate.

TextENGNew: Today in particular was perfectly clear.
The cloudless ultramarine sky was certainly beautiful, in its way -- but for students who had no choice but to sit through tedious classes indoors, the appreciation only went so far.
"If only summer weren't so hot, it'd be a pretty pleasant season."
"...Then it wouldn't be summer."
Tamako, who was on classroom duty today, carried a freshly filled vase in both hands and set it down on the small desk beside the lockers with a little grunt of effort.
She then threw the windows wide open in hopes of catching a breeze, but... the air that drifted in was lukewarm and only made the room more stifling.
...Despite it still being morning, the cicadas in the schoolyard were already performing their deafening chorus.
Someone once told me their lives above ground last only a few weeks. Given that, I suppose I couldn't begrudge them for living those short days with such spirited enthusiasm.
Still, I wished they'd at least consider the volume... and maybe pick a designated time and place for their racket.
Too much to ask? Yeah, probably.
"Haah, it's so hoooot..."
"Yeah, it really... Hey, hold on, Chisato. You're a girl -- stop fanning your skirt like that."
"Huh? It's fine. It's not like anyone's dying to peek at mine, right?"
"Well, you're not wrong."
"Hey, you're supposed to deny that!"
"Yeah, yeah... Haah."
Tamako shrugged with an exasperated sigh at the absurdity of it all, retreated to her own desk, and began fanning herself with a pencil board.
It seemed the heat had sapped even her energy for retorts today.
Her sharp tongue could be a handful, but being ignored entirely was a bit lonely too.
"Heeey, Chisa. You in there?"
Just then, a voice called out to me from outside the classroom. I sluggishly turned to look and saw Akira standing at the entrance.
"Oh, Akira-chan. Good morning."
"...? Oh, good morning."
Akira walked over to us, and for just a moment a look of '...huh?' flickered across his face as he glanced around.
But perhaps noticing my knowing smirk, he quickly composed himself and slipped back into his usual poker face with a casual '...Morning, Chisa.'
"Mornin', Akira. What brings you here so early?"
"Don't give me that 'what brings you here.' You still haven't returned my English-Japanese dictionary. I need it for first period reading, so hand it over."
"Oops, Chii-chan totally forgot. Hang on a sec, let me see..."
Bending down to peer inside my desk seemed like too much effort, so I rummaged around blindly with my hand, trying to identify things by touch.
...The textbooks I'd been leaving in there were starting to pile up. What a pain, but maybe I should take them home today.
"Honestly, Chisato -- when you borrow something, it's proper manners to return it yourself."
"Oh, come on. Akira and I have that kind of relationship. ...Here you go."
"Don't say things that'll give people the wrong idea. Sheesh."
"...Wait, this isn't mine."
"Hm? Oh, you're right."
I looked at the dictionary Akira had thrust back at me and realized my mistake.
Its cover was battered and the pages were thoroughly worn, as if someone had used it to within an inch of its life. It was probably a hand-me-down inherited from some older acquaintance.
changeReason: Significant difference in content length suggests added or omitted information in the new translation.

TextENGNew: The publisher was different from our school's designated edition, too. ...In other words, this was *her* dictionary.
"This one looks really well-used. Is it your... no, it couldn't be."
"How rude. ...You're right, though. That one's Natsumi's."
"Yours is this one. Here, take it already."
"'Take it already'...? And why on earth do you have two copies of the same dictionary?"
"Well, Natsumi's dictionary has sticky notes and highlights everywhere, so it was way easier to use. I kind of strong-armed her into lending it to me when I was studying for midterms here."
"...Chisa, I'm never lending you my dictionary again."
With that curt declaration, he snatched the dictionary roughly from my outstretched hand.
But -- predictable as ever -- Akira stared at Natsumi's dictionary in his other hand for a few seconds before reluctantly handing it back with just a hint of longing.
"So you've been borrowing that dictionary since last week?"
"Hmm, I guess? She said it was fine since she has an old one at home."
"That's not the point, is it? Even though midterms are over, you're still borrowing her notes too. If you keep taking advantage of her kindness, you're going to put her in an awkward position."

"But Natsumi's English notes are amazing -- she writes down the teacher's supplementary explanations and everything, in such incredible detail. Oh, but I help her out with math, so it evens out, right?"
"That's still not the point. Honestly..."
Tamako said that with an air of exasperation, but people have their strengths and weaknesses.
Rather than floundering in subjects you're weak at, it's far more efficient to build on each other's strengths and help each other out. (Excuses, excuses.)

Besides, while Natsumi's notes were admittedly a bit harder to read than an honor student like Tamako's, she'd marked all the parts she didn't understand and even written down what she'd learned from asking the teacher questions.
For me, they were far more thorough and helpful than any study guide, and they'd been a huge asset for the exam just a few days ago.
...Well, I had to admit that when I borrowed them, I'd been pretty pushy about it. Even accounting for all our give-and-take over the years, a mere ice cream wouldn't be enough to make it up to her.
Maybe next time I go shopping near the station, I'll look for something cute to give her as a thank-you. ...Yeah, I'll do that.
"Oh, by the way..."
"Natsumi isn't here yet."
"I-I wasn't even going to ask that!"
His face flushed red at having his question answered before he could even finish asking it.
...His protestations aside, the way he was floundering made it painfully obvious what he'd been about to say.
"Yeah, yeah, I can read you like a book. You were scanning the whole classroom looking for her when you walked in. ...Dead giveaway."
"Th-that's not true. It's just..."
"Just?"
"...Never mind."
Unable to come up with a suitable excuse, he pursed his lips and fell silent.
Oops, I may have teased him a bit too much. ...If I push any further, Akira will just sulk and head back to his own classroom.
Natsumi was running late, but she should be here any minute now. I was sure she'd want to see Akira and chat, so I needed to keep him here.
"She mentioned she'd be a little late, but she should be here soon. The warning bell hasn't even rung yet -- why not wait here?"
"L-like I said, I'm not..."
"Natsumi stopped by the hospital on her way to school today. It's the Saeki General Hospital -- Chisato's family's place -- so I don't think she'll be too long."
"Huh? The hospital...? Is she sick?"
Tamako shot me a sideways glance and shrugged as if to say, 'See what I mean?'
Honestly... He puts on this act of not caring, but one little trick like that and the truth comes tumbling out. He's transparent to a fault.
Well, it just goes to show how *special* that girl is to him.
"No, no, it's nothing like that. She's just accompanying her grandmother for a routine checkup. The old lady might get lost on her own, so Natsumi's escorting her partway."
"Oh, is that all..."
"Good news, Akira-kun. Your precious Natsumi-chan is perfectly fine."
"I-I already told you it's not like that!"
"Now, now. But Natsumi really is such a kind person, doing things like that. Don't you agree, Akira-chan?"
"Ngh... I-I guess so."
Tamako cut in to smooth things over, and when she asked for his agreement, Akira scratched the tip of his nose and nodded.
That had been his tell for hiding embarrassment ever since we were kids. ...Should I clue him in someday?
"She's so attentive. Just the other day, she took an elderly stranger by the hand to help him across the crosswalk."
"She mentioned wanting to become a caregiver someday, and honestly, it seems like her true calling. She's got a great reputation at the care center where she works part-time, right?"
"Yeah. The head caretaker, Yamase-san, always praises her for being such a hard worker and so thoughtful. Man, I'm the one who introduced her to the job, so I'm pretty proud myself. Ahahaha!"
"...Don't go bragging about it."
"What do you mean? I'm that girl's guardian, you know! So naturally I'm proud of the fruits of my hands-on mentorship!"
"...More like you're a cautionary example."
"Ahahaha!"
I laughed it off as a joke, but... that girl really was a tireless hard worker. Honestly, even I was impressed.
I'd heard that when she first transferred from the countryside to our college-prep school, she'd struggled terribly to keep up with the fast-paced classes.
But she studied late into the night every single day, diligently keeping up with her prep work and review... until she'd finally caught up with the rest of us.
She paid close attention in class, took thorough notes, and asked the teacher question after question whenever she didn't understand something.

No teacher in the world would be unhappy with such an earnest, dedicated attitude. That's why even our homeroom teacher Ogata -- a grade-obsessed snob if ever there was one -- had taken a particular shine to Natsumi.
And even though she refused to believe me when I told her, far from looking down on her, the overwhelming majority of her classmates actually held her in high regard.
She might have been a bit clumsy, sure, but she more than made up for it with her sincere, wholehearted effort. ...That was my best friend.
changeReason: The level of certainty expressed differs significantly between old and new translations.

TextENGNew: "...Don't you ever get jealous of Kimiyoshi-san?"
A classmate had once asked me that spiteful question.
...I'd laughed through my nose. Sorry, but our relationship isn't anything so flimsy.
We talked about everything together, confided in each other... sometimes we even fought, but we always made up right away.
It wasn't all fun and happiness -- we understood each other's pain and sorrow too, thought about what we could do for each other, and shared our burdens.
We'd overcome mountains and walls together that no ordinary person could fathom. She was the greatest companion I could ask for.
So while I felt proud to call her my friend, I never harbored any dark feelings about being compared to her.
I believed from the bottom of my heart that my bond with Natsumi was every bit as strong as the ones I shared with my childhood friends, Tamako and Akira.
Yes... my role was to help that girl hold on to the happiness she had right now.
So for Natsumi's sake, I would do anything, no matter what...
"Chisato...?"
"Hm? What is it, Tamako?"
"Is something wrong? You suddenly went quiet..."
"Nah, it's nothing. ...Oh."
At that moment, the door at the back of the classroom rattled open. Then I heard a slightly breathless, sweet voice -- someone had come running.
"Good morning~!"
"Ah, speak of the devil. Morning, Natsu--"
At that moment.
History was made.
No, wait -- history stopped.
Well, okay, maybe that's a bit of an exaggeration... but we all froze on the spot, staring at the doorway, completely lost for words.
"...G-good morning, Chisato-chan, Tamako-chan. ...Oh, um... Toudou-kun, good morning to you too."
Tamako and Akira had their eyes wide open, utterly lost for words. ...I was surely making the exact same face.
It wasn't just us, either -- every single student in the classroom had stopped mid-conversation... and simply stood there, frozen, staring at her in stunned silence.
"Oh... um, what's wrong, everyone...?"
That adorable voice.
That pleasant ring to it.
And that sparkling, friendly face.
...Without question, these belonged to the best friend I knew.
And yet.
Huh... wait... whaaat?!
"I-I knew it... Th-this hair looks weird, doesn't it?"
Looking crestfallen, the 'beautiful girl' before me lowered her gaze and peered up at us timidly, trying to read our expressions.
And seeing that expression... as endearing as a small frightened animal... I...
*snap*
...felt something inside me break, and opened my mouth.
"...Natsumi."
"Yes?"
"Please marry me!!"
"Kyaaah?!"
Heedless of Natsumi's shriek, I practically pounced on her, wrapping my arms around her petite frame.
"What? What what? Whaaaaat?!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1054. Can something this adorable actually exist in this world?@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1055. Is this the pinnacle of human craftsmanship?!"
"No, that's not it!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1057. This is the very image of a fairy -- an angel!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1058. A creation straight from the realm of fantasy!!"
"Ch-Ch-Ch-Chisato-chan??"
"Ahhh... how inadequate words are!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1060. To look upon this and find no expression beyond 'cute,' 'beautiful,' and 'lovely'!"
"Japanese alone cannot do this beauty justice!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1062. Line up every word from English, French, German, Russian, and Kamchatkan, and you still wouldn't capture even half of it!"
"Fine then! If you insist I sum it up in a single phrase, I'll stand at the center of the world and declare it!@k@|@y@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1064. That's right -- 'I love you!!'"
"Wh-what are you saying...? Hyaaahh?!"
"Yes... yes, I've finally realized it!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1066. What I've been searching for all this time!"
"Was it wealth?@k@|@y@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1068. Or fame?@k@|@y@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1069. No! I haven't endured this cruel world for anything so trivial!"
"What I've wanted is this -- one true love!!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1071. And that is YOU, Natsumi Kimiyoshi!!!"
"H-hold on, Chisato?!"

"Silence!@k@|@y@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1073. Stay back! Don't touch! Don't come near!@k@|@y@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1074. My mind is made up!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1075. I won't hand this precious creature over to anyone!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1076. Even if you offered me Japan's entire national budget, the answer would be a resounding 'No thank you!'"
"Here and now, I make my declaration!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1078. I hereby renounce my womanhood!!"
"Wha--?@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1031. H-hey... Chisa?!"
"Out of my way, Akira!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1080. This girl is my bride now!"
"Come, Natsumi!!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1082. We're off to the Netherlands, where same-sex marriage is recognized!!"
"Y-you absolute idiot!!"
"Bwurghk?!"
A sharp blow struck the back of my head. In the instant the pain made me involuntarily loosen my grip, Tamako pulled Natsumi away and into her own arms.
"Ow, that huuurt~. What was that for, Tamako?!"
"That's my line! What are you doing, spouting nonsense like that in front of everyone?!"
"What nonsense?! I was simply acting on my honest desires and wishes!! What's wrong with that?!"
"Everything, from the tips of your toes to the top of your head!!@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1034. ...Oh."
Tamako then turned to face Natsumi, whom she'd ended up holding close in all the commotion.
...Hey, Tamako? Why is your face turning red? Am I imagining things, or is there something different about the way you're looking at Natsumi right now?!
"Um... Tamako-chan?"
"Oh... S-sorry, Natsumi. The sudden makeover just caught me completely off guard..."
"So... it doesn't look weird?"
"Not at all! It's not just 'not weird' -- it's incredibly cute! I thought some princess had walked in!"
"Well, for the record, I'm a perfectly respectable young lady myself."
"Pipe down, you bargain-bin young lady. You're ruining the image, so hush."
"Hey, Tamakoooo!! And let go of her already!!"
"Mmm~, so lovely, so cute~! Oh, I just want to take her home with me! That's fine with you, right, Natsumi? I won't accept a refusal!"
"Huh? ...K-kyaah?!"
With that, Tamako completely ignored me and, with a blissful 'hanyaaan~' sound practically emanating from her, pulled Natsumi into a tight embrace.
Hey Tamako, that's MY job! You're supposed to be the one holding me back! Don't just swap positions without permission!!
"Oh... T-Toudou-kun. Umm..."
"Huh? Oh... uh, wh-what?"
Meanwhile... Akira, left behind and shell-shocked by the commotion, was finally brought back to his senses when Natsumi called out to him.
The instant he snapped out of it, his face flushed so red it looked like it might boil, and -- pathetically enough -- he averted his gaze to escape Natsumi's eyes.
changeReason: Significant difference in content length suggests added or omitted information in the new translation.

TextENGNew: "Um, does this hairstyle look... weird...?"
"N-no, I... umm..."
Oh boy, he really is hopeless in situations like this. All he had to do was say 'it looks nice' and he'd have scored major points.
Good grief, what am I going to do with him...
changeReason: Significant difference in content length suggests added or omitted information in the new translation.

TextENGNew: "Whoa there, Akiraaa -- don't tell me you don't like it~?"

"What a disappointment, right, Natsumi? Akira's been looking at too many beauties in his art that he's forgotten what a real one looks like. Well, I suppose when your standards are as lofty as Akira's, it can't be helped~."
"Th-that's not true at all! I-it's beautiful!!"
Akira blurted that out in reflexive response to my transparent provocation. Hearing it, Natsumi's eyes flew open so wide you could practically hear the pop, and her face turned crimson.
Seeing her reaction, he let out an '...oh' as he realized his slip -- but the words, once spoken, couldn't be taken back.
"Uh, um, well... I-I, uhh..."
"...Toudou-kun?"
"Th-that hairstyle... it's so... so beautiful that... I was just really surprised. I didn't know what to say... s-so, umm...!"
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1020.Thank you."
"--?!@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1043. R-right..."

Stumbling over his words, Akira had managed to string together every embarrassing line he could muster -- and upon receiving the reward of her smile at point-blank range, his face went scarlet and he fell completely silent.
...Well, for a blockhead like him, that was a Herculean effort. He actually made it to the end. Good for him.
"E-excuse me... Kimiyoshi-san?"
"Your hair is so beautiful... Could I maybe touch it, just for a moment?"
"S-sure... go ahead..."
"...Wow, it's so smooth and silky! You could be a model -- it's gorgeous..."
"Hey, what kind of shampoo do you use? How do you get it so beautiful?"
"U-umm... well..."
Other girls swarmed around Natsumi in the same fashion, bombarding her with questions and squealing with excitement.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to shion, altering the specificity of the passage.

TextENGNew: Some of them weren't usually close to her at all, but that's how strikingly gorgeous her hair looked today -- it commanded everyone's attention.
Long, beautiful hair has always been the eternal dream of girls, after all.
And for the boys, even more so...
"Hey Akira, this is bad, you know...?"
"Huh? Wh-what do you mean...?"
"Look around at the boys' faces. ...They're practically drooling."
Akira snapped to attention and surveyed the room... then stared in open-mouthed disbelief.
...At the edge of his line of sight, Maeda, the ace of the volleyball team, had turned around in his seat and sat gaping in this direction.
And Yokoyama, the leading candidate for brass band conductor, kept stealing glances at Natsumi while pretending to chat with a friend.
Both were quite handsome and popular with the girls.
On top of that, Mimura from the student council was staring in a daze with flushed cheeks. ...If I recalled correctly, there'd been a rumor about him being featured in a fashion magazine recently.
As for the rest of the rank and file... well, that goes without saying.
"Wha--? H-hey...!"
"My, my... looks like a bidding war is about to break out any day now."
"A b-bidding... war?!"
"Of course. If even the girls are making this much fuss, you think the boys are going to just sit back and watch?"
In just a few days -- no, perhaps by tomorrow morning -- her shoe locker was going to be overflowing.
Some guys might even try to use me or Tamako as go-betweens. ...Well, there was no way I'd lift a finger for anyone that spineless.
"So are you okay with this, Akira? You're really fine just standing by and watching?"
"Wh-what are you getting at?!"
"Exactly what you think. Natsumi can't say no to people. ...If some good-looking guy confesses to her, she might just accept on the spot~."
"Wha?!"
"And with this, her popularity is going to skyrocket. She already had quite a few secret admirers, but it's like her stock price just went through the roof."
"...R-really?!"
Oh my, he's panicking now.
Faced with this shocking reality, he no longer had the composure to keep up pretenses or hide his expression. He swallowed my bait hook, line, and sinker, turning to me with eyes wide in alarm.
"Oh my~? Could it be that you thought Natsumi was a safe bet, and you could afford to take your sweet time~?"
"Th-that's not true! Oh, no... I mean, the thing is, I, umm..."
"Just be honest, Akira. If you're going to make a move, now's the time. That much is certain."
He must have been feeling a genuine sense of crisis, because instead of dismissing my words as fishy nonsense like he usually did (how rude!), Akira was listening with solemn attention.
Then again, seeing the situation unfolding before him, I suppose it was only natural...
...Right on cue, the warning bell rang out as if to quell the commotion.
First period was about to begin.
"Alright, Akira, class is starting soon. Get yourself back to your own classroom."
"R-right..."
"...Haah, can't be helped. Come back at lunch. I'll have a plan for you."
I whispered that into Akira's ear as he gazed anxiously at Natsumi, then gave him a pat on the shoulder and shooed him out of the classroom.
...Honestly, these two babies need constant supervision.
"...Chisato."
"Hm...? What is it, Tama-san?"
"...@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1047.No, it's nothing."
With that, Tamako went to calm down the still-buzzing girls, then returned to her desk. I watched her retreating back, shrugged slightly, and took my seat.
Don't worry, Tamako. This is the conclusion I chose...
Glaring at the blank white canvas on his easel, Akira let out yet another deep sigh -- he'd lost count of how many that made since entering the art room.
"This is hopeless..."
He'd planned to start on his summer assignment painting today, but... he couldn't come up with a single motif.
Or rather, there was no way he could.
Akira's mind was completely consumed by something that had nothing to do with painting -- something that, for him, was close to a monumental decision.
"...Why did I go and ask for something like that..."
Besieged by belated regret and self-loathing, he buried his head in his hands.
...It really had been pure momentum.

During lunch break, Chisato and Tamako had dragged him up to the rooftop and bombarded him with advice that bordered on intimidation -- 'Confess to her now!' -- and before he knew it, he'd been steamrolled into accepting their help.
"C-confess?! H-hold on, that's not what I..."

"It was crazy during break, I tell you. The moment class ended, a bunch of boys came up to Natsumi with excuses like 'Can you help me with this part?' -- just trying to get close to her. That's never happened before."
"R-really...?!"
Without even questioning whether Chisato's story was true, the scene she described took shape in Akira's mind.
A classmate casually starting a conversation, and Natsumi responding with a smile. It was an opportunity someone in a different class, like him, could never have.
...Although, Chisato and Tamako both recognized that Akira probably wouldn't be able to do that even if they were in the same class.

"Well, honestly, Natsumi didn't really seem to know how to handle them...@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1125. If things like this keep happening in the future, some idiots who won't take no for an answer might get the wrong idea and set their sights on her."
"...W-wrong idea?"
"Like "Does this mean you like me?"@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1049. Akira-chan, if you leave things as they are, they'll beat you to the punch."
"W-well..."
"It's all right, be confident!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1127. Natsumi definitely likes you!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1128. ...For now, anyway."
Akira quietly lamented how Chisato's support was always followed up with a gut punch. ...If she would just leave out those last few words, she'd be a pretty great person.
"B-but, I... umm..."

"Come on... we're not some bystanders lighting a fire for no reason.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1130. We're doing our utmost to help because we're your childhood friends.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1131. ...I'm telling you, we're being sincere, okay?"
"...Plus, Akira-chan, you've always liked Natsumi, right?@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1051. Be honest.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1052. ...Otherwise, someone else might join in the running and scoop her up."
He suddenly looked up at the ceiling.
Without even closing his eyes, a vision of Natsumi's embarrassed smile from earlier this morning came to mind.
"It was... so cute.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1071. Really..."
Rather than simply falling in love, it would be right to say he was moved by just how lovely she was.
...That's right, he admitted it. When asked whether or not he liked her, he definitely did.
But it wasn't just some casual thought because she was cute.
And it wasn't just because she got along so well with Chisato and Tamako.
It was more than that... Akira was attracted to Natsumi for stronger, deeper reasons.
...He was drawing an ordinary landscape painting depicting the scene outside the school window.
He wasn't planning to submit this illustration anywhere.
At first it was nothing more than a distraction... but he really took a liking to it as he worked his brush, and became fully immersed in it, creating a work for his own sake in the truest sense.
So much that even Akira himself thought it was the ultimate in self-indulgence, and a bit masochistic.
So after he finished the painting, he hung it on the wall in the corner of the art room, but it just sat their gathering dust, with no one paying it any real attention.
But, that moment...
"I've never seen a picture so beautiful before..."
It was the transfer student, who Chisato was escorting to the art room for the first time... Natsumi Kimiyoshi.
She looked up at the picture Chisato was pointing at while saying "Akira drew that," then suddenly let out a sigh...

"That's amazing.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1026. I can't believe a picture this incredible was drawn by someone my age...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1027. It's the scenery just outside the window here, isn't it?"
"Ah, yeah..."

"I'm not so good at putting things into words... but the contrast between the blue of the sky and the red of the sunset off in the distance is very beautiful.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1029. It feels so much warmer, gentler, and happier than the actual scene in person..."
"Well... uhh... Huh?!"
Just as he began to speak up, a line of tears streaked down Natsumi's face as she gazed forward.
Akira was timidly listening to her impressions, but he was surprised when he noticed that reaction.
"Umm, uhh...@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1076. I don't really understand, but... sorry."
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1031. Ah, i-it's not like that!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1032. This is just the first time looking at a picture has made me feel so moved..."
Akira lost his voice as soon as he heard Natsumi say that.
He had been praised countless times before.
People told him his work was beautiful.
But... for someone to be moved to tears.
Never before had someone looked at one of his pictures and taken it so deeply to heart...

"I don't know much about art, and I'm no good at painting, so I can't give a good critique... but I do honestly think this picture is amazing.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1034. Because I've never seen such a beautiful sunset sky before..."
...He was delighted.
He was thrilled that a girl he was slightly acquainted with, who was roughly the same age as him, was willing to go so far to praise one of his paintings.
He had plenty of friends and acquaintances who were proud of him for winning prizes.
But... Natsumi was the first one to show such a simple and sincere reaction to seeing one of his paintings...
"...I'm really glad I moved to Kakiuchi."
"Because if someone from this city is able to create such an incredible painting, I have a feeling the rest of the people here are incredible too."
"...No, it's not just a feeling.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1038. The fact that I made such wonderful friends right after moving here is proof of that."
"...I've really grown fond of this town, ever since I moved here.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1040. But, I'm sure I'll end up loving it even more in the future."
"It's a weird thing to say, but looking at this picture gives me the courage to believe that... That's how it feels."
"...@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1081.Thanks.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1082. If you like it that much, then I'm glad I painted it."
And... Natsumi said it was "good," to the point that the sunset sky moved her to tears.
In truth, that sunset was the part Akira fretted the most over while drawing that painting.
That's why he instinctively broke out into an impassioned speech that left Chisato, Tamako, and even himself surprised by how talkative he became...

"...This color here was giving me a hard time.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1084. I didn't want it to be an ominous red, but something more vivid and full of life...@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1085. I wanted to convey a sense of finding hope, so..."
"Wow... is that so...?"
"Umm... Kimiyoshi...san.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1087. If I paint another picture, would you like to see it?"
"Eh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1044. S-sure..."
That was the precise moment when Akira became completely fascinated by the girl named Natsumi Kimiyoshi... He realized that now.
And, even nearly a year later, his feelings hadn't faded at all. In fact, they'd grown even stronger...
There were other nice girls.
Cute girls weren't all that uncommon either.
But... she was a particularly "special" girl.
"...Natsumi is such a good girl."
Even though Chisato was always playful and full of smiles, when Akira asked her what kind of girl Natsumi was, she gave him a completely serious and straightforward answer.
At first, he didn't understand the meaning of that simple assessment... but now, he knew.
She really was a "good girl."
She cared deeply for others and tried her hardest to understand them... She was a girl you could easily talk to anytime, anywhere.
It must've been her kind personality that made him feel that way...
"...Toudou-kun?"
Just then, a voice called out from the entrance of the art room, so Akira stood up straight and looked back.
...Natsumi cautiously stepped inside, likely here because Chisato and Tamako told her to come.
"S-sorry for calling you here.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1090. You didn't have any trouble finding your way here, did you?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1047. N-no...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1048. I've come here plenty of times before..."
"Oh... r-right.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1092. S-sorry..."
Seeing the bitter smile on her face, he immediately felt regret and apologized to her.
...His sense of time seemed skewed since he was thinking about that day they met so long ago.
Even so, Akira steadied his breathing and encouraged himself... then turned toward Natsumi.
"...Toudou-kun?"
"Listen.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1094. Umm... Kimiyoshi."
Natsumi quizzically tilted her head to the side while gazing up at Akira.
...He always thought she was cute.
But with her hair flowing like this, combined with that expression on her face, she looked several times more charming than usual, so Akira's mind went completely blank and he froze up.
"Um... there's... uhh..."
"S-so... umm..."
Despite being irritated by his lack of confidence, Akira tried to inspire himself by searching the corners of his mind for the right words.
He had to say it.
He had to tell her something.
Even if he didn't get a good response... it would be far too sad to let it end without saying anything.
Yes, he didn't need anyone to tell him that. He completely understood that...
Still, as the choking air continued flowing between them... his reckless valor slowly diminished.
What should he do?
Should he say something?
In the end, would it be better to say nothing?
If she refused his confession right here... would he never be able to talk to her again?
Up until now, he always had a reserved space for her gentle smile and cozy demeanor.
The fear of losing that all at once was digging deeper into his heart... and his voice wouldn't come out at all, like his throat was completely clogged.
...But, he had to say it.
He had to at least say something.
Either way, Natsumi was waiting for Akira to say something.
What's more, her face gradually shifted away from her earlier dubious expression and started turning red, with her mouth tightly closed and a meek look on her face.
...Perhaps she was starting to guess what Akira was trying to say.
But if he tried to dodge the subject or run away here, surely she'd be either shocked or appalled...
"...K-Kimiyoshi."
"...@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1101.I... want to know more about you, Kimiyoshi..."
And so, he spoke up.
Akira finally squeezed out the courage to say something...
"Toudou...kun?"
"I want to know.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1103. Your favorite books, your favorite movies...@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1104. What kind of guys you like... and, umm, what you think about guys like me...?"
"And... if you'd be able to like a guy like me... That's what I want to know.@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1106. Because... Kimiyoshi... I like you."
After fighting so hard to squeeze those words out... Akira took a deep breath.
...He finally said it.
At last, he told her.
And, Natsumi...
After listening to Akira's words, she fell silent... and gazed down at the floor.
...What was she thinking about?
What was she going to say?
"I'm sorry"?
"I was so surprised"?
"Thank you"? ...No, that would be far too pretentious.

But, it might be discourteous to expect an immediate answer to a sudden confession. Thinking that, he started to call out to Natsumi again to say "You can answer whenever you're ready" ...but just then.
...Something came from her eyes, hidden behind her bangs... and fell to the floor.
"I-I'm sorry, Kimiyoshi!@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1109. I, umm..."
"Toudou-kun."
"Huh?"
And then.
He started feeling regret, wondering if something he said could've hurt her feelings.
Akira was so upset he was about to cry, but... Natsumi gently grabbed his hand... and then spoke up in a faint voice.
"You're okay with me?"
"You're really fine with me... Toudou-kun?"
"Kimiyoshi...?"
"This isn't a dream?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1065. Toudou-kun, you're really telling me this...?"
As she said that.
Tears were gushing from both Natsumi's eyes... and she had a joyous smile on her face, from the bottom of her heart.
"Kimiyoshi...@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1114. Uh, I..."
"I... like you, too.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1067. Toudou-kun, I love you..."
...Huh?
R-really?
This isn't a dream?
She's not lying, or joking around? ...No, he knew she wasn't the sort of person to do that.
Then... then...?!
"So... then, umm..."
"...Yeah."
While still crying and smiling, Natsumi nodded to him.
In that moment, his dark thoughts and heavy emotions vanished all at once.
And they were replaced with... first of all, bewilderment.
But also... enough sheer joy to overpower that.
"...Will you... go out with me?"
"O-of course...!@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1118. I swear I'll treasure you!@k@vS14/39/VT4Bb_akira1119. More than anyone else...!!"
"...Thanks."
Tears came spilling from Natsumi's eyes once again.
As he handed her a handkerchief, Akira vowed to himself... that he would remember Natsumi's smile right now for the rest of his life.
"...Think it's going well?"
"I'm sure it is.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1134. I'm not worried at all."
I took a drink from the milk coffee I bought at the vending machine while resting my elbows on the rooftop railing.
There apparently wasn't much left, because the palm-sized paper carton immediately shrunk, and the straw let out a loud slurping noise.

"Ever since we first introduced them, I could tell there was something different in Natsumi's eyes when she looked at Akira-chan.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1055. But it felt like she never had the confidence to tell us."
"...You're overthinking it.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1136. Well, that is one of the nice things about her."
Natsumi probably knew that we'd help her if she told us... and I think that's why she didn't say anything.
If she had confessed her feelings for Akira to us, naturally we'd push her to tell him.
But... in the off-chance that Akira shot her down, it could make things so awkward that he'd never talk to me or Tamako ever again.
In the end, her personal love interests could hurt the childhood friendship that the three of us shared... That must've been what she was so worried about.
So, instead... she did her best to keep her thoughts a secret, and didn't even realize we already knew.
Her thoughts made her so desperate that she couldn't see what was around her. I completely understood that feeling.
But... that's okay. That's why I wanted to do my best for her, too...
"At any rate, it seems like Natsumi had no idea that Akira-chan liked her, too.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1057. I guess if you focus too much on other things, you don't notice things about yourself?"
"Haha, it was just a matter of timing.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1139. The way Akira felt about her was so obvious."
Right... we realized how both of them felt really early on.
And we knew that both of them were at a complete stalemate, oblivious to each other's feelings.
But, the reason we kept quiet was...
"Hey, Chisato,@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1059. are you really okay with this?"
"...With what?"
"What do you think...? Your own feelings.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1061. The truth is, you..."
"Yeah.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1142. I'm fine with this."
Tamako was checking up on me because she was concerned. But, I answered her plainly and completely from the heart.

"This was my own selfish gamble from the start.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1144. On whether it'd be her or Akira who confessed to the other.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1145. ...And if either of them actually managed to do it, then I was planning to back off and keep quiet.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1146. I told you that before, didn't I?"
"I remember that.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1063. ...But in the end, you actually pushed Akira-chan to step forward today, didn't you?@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1064. Doesn't that violate your first rule?"

"That's because if I continued to keep quiet, someone in the class would confess to Natsumi sooner or later, wouldn't they?@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1148. And knowing how she is, I can't imagine her turning someone down..."
And she's the type that would try to keep on smiling happily even if she wasn't going out with Akira.
But... that's not what Natsumi wants.
Setting aside my feelings for Akira, I really did like Natsumi.
And, above all...
"Chisato...?"
"...Oh, it's nothing."
I can't tell Tamako... I just can't say it.
But, ever since I learned her "secret" a little while ago... I truly wanted her to be happy. To smile from the bottom of her heart.
I'll do whatever it takes to make Natsumi happy...
That's why, even though I've been holding back these feelings for so many years... I'm not sure if you could call it love, but it felt so much weaker in comparison.
"What on earth happened?@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1067. Why, all of a sudden...?"
"...Even I think it's selfish.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1151. I realize that much."
"But there's one thing I'm certain of.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1153. ...I will never regret this decision."
"Introducing Natsumi to Akira, giving them a chance to spend time together... all of it.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1155. This might be unusual for me, but I do think this is the best choice."
"Chisato..."
Tamako stared at me for a while... but before long, she smiled and relaxed her shoulders.
We've been friends for a long time, so I'm sure she was able to recognize it. ...She asked nothing more, and simply nodded to convey "...I understand."

"Anyway... this is game, set, and match.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1157. Well, it's to be expected that the favorite would win, and it would end without incident.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1158. ...And that's fine."
"...Right.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1071. Things have calmed down... All's well that ends well."
"...@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1160.I'm sorry, Tamako."
I submissively bowed my head.
First of all, she was in the hardest position in this situation. She was aware of how both of us felt, so she couldn't do anything to support Natsumi.
Someone who didn't know the circumstances might consider her a cold person... but it's actually the opposite.
She must've hated herself for being unable to do anything for Natsumi.
But... that's all over now. Finally, it's all in the past...
"She told us before... I think it was last fall when she said it.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1162. She said she really loved us."
"Yeah, I remember."
"Chisato-chan, Tamako-chan.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1072. ...Could I talk to you for a minute?"
After school one day, we were eating crepes in front of the station and chatting about nonsense like usual.
...But all of a sudden, Natsumi turned toward us and said that.
"...What's wrong, Natsumi?@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1165. Did something happen?"
Her mouth was shut tight, like she was deeply thinking about something. The fresh cream in the crepe she held in both hands melted and spilled down to the ground.
"Natsumi...?"
And, while she continued looking lost in thought, I pulled a tissue out of my pocket to wipe her dirty hands, but just then... Natsumi raised her head and looked straight at us.
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1075.I know this is a weird thing to say now of all times, but... I'm sorry.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1076. I... I'm... really grateful to the both of you.."
"You've been so kind and caring since I moved here from the countryside.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1078. Even today, you've been taking me to all kinds of places and telling me so many fun stories..."
"...Isn't that..."
Obvious? You're our friend.
I smiled and started to say that... but as soon as I saw the serious look on Natsumi's face, I held it down. I could tell right away that this was no time for flippant remarks.

"But... there's nothing I can do for the two of you.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1080. I can hardly keep up in our conversations. You have so many interesting topics to share, but I can't offer anything for the two of you."
"So, I'm... really sorry... I'm always worried about that..."
"Natsumi..."
Tamako seemed a bit confused and turned toward me. I probably had the same look on my face, too.
...There's no need for her to worry about that at all.
It's not like hanging out with Natsumi is volunteer work.
It's fun to chat and play with her, so we just do it.
But... somehow, I understood.
The reason Natsumi is so worried... is because she feels out of place around us.
And that was causing her to develop an inferiority complex.
(Ah, come to think of it...)
When I looked at Natsumi's face, I suddenly remembered. ...She was sitting around looking dejected like this on the first day she transferred here.
She didn't know anybody in the class back then, and she seemed lonely.
She didn't have the courage to talk to anyone. She was like a frightened little animal.
It was nothing strange or special. Anyone would feel that way after being thrown into a strange new place on their own.
And that out-of-place feeling gradually twists into discomfort, then to loneliness.
To the point where you feel inferior and wonder if you should even be there...
(But even if that's the case...)
If you keep living in that atmosphere, you'll also hesitate about how to interact with others. It takes courage to speak up. It was the same way for us in the beginning.
Normally, I wouldn't want to get involved. ...But for some reason, I wanted to break that vicious cycle, so I decided to try a little harder and talk to her.
I didn't take it that seriously at first, figuring that'd act as the trigger for a kindred spirit to reach out to her and get along.
But, it far exceeded my expectations.
"Heheheheh... Aa-ha-ha-hah!!"
"Mwahaha...!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1172. I like you, Kimiyoshi-san!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1173. You're a nice character!!@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1174. Ahahahaha!!"
"...Eh...?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1084. U-uh...?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1085. Huh? Huh?"
I wasn't making fun of her, nor was I looking down on her.
I just found that slightly off-kilter yet passionate reaction really refreshing and amusing.
And that brought to mind memories of my beloved little sister... She was so similar to Kaori...

"...Sorry for suddenly laughing like that.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1076. Anyway, if there's ever something bothering you, don't hesitate to speak up, okay?@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1077. Because we'll do what we can to help."
"Huh?@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1087. Uh, umm... but..."
"Don't be shy. We're friends now.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1176. So let's get along, okay?"
...Since then, I've been getting along and talking with Natsumi, and started taking her all sorts of places, like today.
Any time she was confused about things in class or how to deal with our teacher, I'd explain it in a funny way, doing my best to reduce the tension.
I told her about food and fashion... about what sort of things are popular in this city, acting as a sort of intermediary out of consideration.
She recently mentioned wanting to buy an anniversary gift for her parents, so I even helped her get a part-time job.
Somehow, I had assumed a guardian role for Natsumi... I felt like a parent. But at first, Tamako apparently thought that behavior was unexpected.

"You always enjoy playing the fool and causing a commotion... but you've never shown this level of compassion for anyone other than me or Akira-chan, have you?@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1079. What on earth is going on?"
"Hmm~?@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1178. Well, some way or another... it just happened."
Honestly, there was no deep reason behind it.
She did give off a very similar vibe to Kaori, so that probably encouraged me a bit.
She's just a little shy, but she's really honest and her reactions when I tease her are always fun.
Listening to us intently and thinking so seriously... doing her best to respond to us.
The more time I spent with Natsumi, the more and more I started to like her.
Some say that the friendships between girls are paper thin, but when it comes to her I can confidently puff my chest out and say that's not the case.
So...
"...Hey, Natsumi?"
"I'm grateful to you too.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1181. For always going along with my selfish whims."
While fondly remembering those days, I gently grabbed Natsumi's hand... and wiped it clean with the tissue.
And I tried to look at her with a smile that conveyed I was being completely serious, instead of joking around like usual.
"Chisato-chan...?"

"Listen, I'm fairly... I mean, I'm really caught up in my assumptions.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1183. There are times when I just push ahead and do whatever I think, without even considering other people's feelings."
"So much so that Tamako even tells me from time to time that I need to watch out for it."
"...Ahaha.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1081. Indeed, I won't deny that."
Tamako shrugged her shoulders with a bitter smile on her face, while rolling up her empty crepe wrapper. And, in a hushed tone, she said to me "...While we're at it..."

"Plus, she's fickle and quick to get excited...@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1083. Because of her selfish and reckless tendencies, Chisato has never really been considerate of anyone in particular up until now."

"Or perhaps others get fed up with her and leave her on their own.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1085. ...So, I was a little surprised when she first said we should make friends with you and take you around with us.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1086. It felt like a trick of my imagination."
"...Really?"
Natsumi's eyes were open wide as she lightly tilted her head.
Perhaps up until now she thought we were particularly considerate people, even amongst our classmates.
But, Tamako aside, I wasn't an especially benevolent person. ...If anything, even I was well aware that I had a strong tendency to avoid troublesome things.
In that sense, Natsumi really was something special to me.
"...But recently, I felt like I understood why.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1088. Natsumi, you may not realize this yourself, but... you really are such a "good girl.""
"No matter how different your values are, no matter if you're not good at something, you always try your best to adapt and understand it."

"...Sometimes you misunderstand and act slightly different from what we might want, but you always put in your best effort to respond.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1091. And because of that... you're absolutely delightful."
"Delightful...?"
"Yes.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1093. There are occasionally times that I'm surprised you don't know something... but Natsumi, just because something is natural for us doesn't mean it is for you, right?"
"Even so, you always take the time to think about our feelings, to accept and understand them.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1095. Even now, you're seriously thinking about what you can do as our friend."
"...That's why we're just as grateful to you."
"...Tamako-chan."
"But, now that you say that... I never noticed that we were making things so hard for you."
"I've gotten carried away and relied on you too much... I've probably made you put up with a lot.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1187. ...Sorry about that."
"N-no way...!@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1096. I'm the one who was always relying on you two. You've always been very considerate of me.@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1097. And yet, I..."
Natsumi once again began looking apologetic, and she rushed to blame herself, like I was saying something outrageous with my self-reflective comments.
...Honestly, it really is just as Tamako said. She's such a "good girl." But...
"Aah, no no, that's not it.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1189. You're welcome to let us know how you truly think and feel... That's what I'm trying to say."
"Because otherwise I'm the only one having a good time, and that's just unfair, right?"
"U-unfair...?"
"Yeah, it's unfair."
Friendship is about looking out for one another, not thinking about how you can repay favors.
...Even so, an excess of kindness that's not in line with your friend's values, circumstances, or intentions could definitely make you feel inferior instead. I've heard something like that before.
It's true that I've done all sorts of things to take care of Natsumi and look out for her as a friend.
But, if she believed those thoughts were coming from a sense of superiority or arrogance, she might feel confused and overwhelmed.
The important thing is to understand how the other person feels. And the best way to do that is to talk openly with one another and share your opinions.
(Although...)
If anything, I'm grateful Natsumi said it herself.
If she hadn't, being the dimwit I am, I would never have realized that I was hurting my best friend...
"So, if you ever feel that I'm going too far, I want you to speak up."

"I may not be able to tell yet whether that's because you're just feeling shy or if you're seriously hurt.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1195. ...But, I'll try my best do a little more to meet your expectations."
"Chisato-chan... But, both of you have..."

"It's fine.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1098. Natsumi, we now have a better understanding of your feelings thanks to what you've told us.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1099. So this time, we want to be able to accept your circumstances and values."
"I'm sure we'll be a bit confused at first, just like you were at the start.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1101. ...But, I want to reciprocate. To show you I understand."
"Tamako-chan..."
"So let's make a rule. This doesn't apply to just me or you, but to all three of us.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1197. Let's be comfortable around each other, to make this a nicer world."
"I want us to share our hardships with one another, help each other, and have fun doing it.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1199. Because after all, we're friends!"
That's right.
Friendship isn't about doing things for your convenience, or imposing your will on others.
It's a relationship where you have fun together and make each other happy. That's friendship.
You can share your worries. There's no need to hesitate.
Because it's not about repaying anything. It's about offering kindness and support without reservation.
By doing that, you're not alone. ...Because that trust and peace of mind allows us all to achieve greater happiness...
"Chisato-chan... Tamako-chan..."
"From now on, if there's anything bothering you, let's discuss it together.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1201. If we feel like one of us is going too far or making mistakes, we'll tell it to their face."

"Sometimes it might not feel very good to do that, but I believe there's something precious about having someone you can talk to.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1203. If we're able to do that, I feel we can get along even better and better."

"That's right.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1103. Well, if you have any trouble with this one, you can always talk to me about it, too.@k@vS14/38/VT4Bb_tama1104. Because I can get the point across to her in a more fatal blow than you could on your own. Okay, Natsumi?"
"...Hold up, Tama-san! Why are you talking about it like I'm the one who screwed something up?@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1205. This is about all of us, right?!"
"Because that's reality."
"What was that?! You showstealer!"
"...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1103.Thanks, you two...@k@vS14/36/VT4Bb_natsu1104. I love you both so much...!"
"Hehe..."
"Ahaha..."
...Looking at it from the outside, it must've been a very naive... very embarrassing sight.
However.
At the time, it felt like... we truly understood each other, as friends...
We've had some arguments since then.
And even if you couldn't call them outright fights, there were certainly times where we had conflicts of opinion that ended up causing issues.
But every time, we'd talk it over, discuss our feelings, apologize, and forgive each other...
We have a strong, firm bond, like childhood friends.

"That's why, from the bottom of my heart, I'm going to do everything I can to help that girl find happiness."

"I admit when we first met, part of me felt she was like a replacement for Kaori.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1212. ...But now, it's different.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1213. She's my friend.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1214. My precious, important, best friend.@k@vS14/37/VT4Bb_chisa1215. I'm not sure how you feel... but I definitely feel that way."
